name: Saudi Arabia
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **SAUDI MBS DAY 8 OF 14 SILENCE ON BOTH UAE OPEC EXIT (May 1) AND MAY 4 STRIKE ON THE GCC OPEC-DEFECTOR.** UAE sent its FOREIGN MINISTER (not its ruler) to the May 5 Gulf leaders' meeting in Jeddah hosted by MBS — deliberate diplomatic distance signal compounds. OPEC+ proceeded with symbolic 188 thousand bpd production adjustment May 3 with the UAE no longer in the cartel. Atlantic Council Derentz framework continuation: UAE's quiet professionalism in handling the May 4 strike (air-defense success, FM not ruler to Jeddah, no direct Iran-naming) is consistent with the May 1 OPEC exit posture — UAE committing to a separate-from-cartel strategy that values its US-Hormuz alignment over GCC unity. The June 7 OPEC ministerial is the structural test for MBS substantive response. Vali Nasr 'Gulf monarchies risk-averse' framework holds: Saudi MBS silence reflects same calculus as UAE air-defense+restraint. Tradawul TASI -0.8% Wed reflects Saudi-UAE bifurcation pricing. Saudi Arabia continues structural Hormuz-export suppression; Aramco backstops Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry trigger UNCHANGED). The 14-day MBS-formal-response trigger (closes May 12) at Day 8 of 14 = increasing evidence of NO good response.

active_triggers.condition: Yanbu pipeline capacity maxed out or sustained attack closes it
Sadara JV ($3.7B debt) defaults at June 15 grace expiry without operational recovery
